mentions.note: Once red-hot software stock; disappointed this evening; part of rotation OUT of software INTO hardware; investors don't want software, only semiconductors right now.
Pure play cybersecurity; included in CIBR top holdings; benefiting from cybersecurity comeback.
Down 38% Q1; cybersecurity play; Wall Street fears AI displacement but unfounded per CrowdStrike's Kurtz and Palo Alto's Arora; prefer CrowdStrike or Palo Alto over Zscaler.
Security software vendor caught in broader enterprise software disruption; losing relevance and pricing power to AI-native approaches.
Caller bought at $250, now $156; Cramer advised hold bounce, lighten up if overweight in cyber; Red Canary acquisition and stock comp concerns valid but stock-based comp should normalize.
